item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a very fun read! Thank you for linking me to this! I enjoyed it a lot! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: we ABSOLUTELY need a 4576 / 4768 crossover at some point

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the kind words! ^-^
I do appreciate it. Tbh, and I'm saying this ahead of time, if you or anybody else wanna take this prickly pal of mine and put him in Aces and Eights, I'd be down for it. 100%.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love this. It's the perfect mixture of weirdness and genuine storytelling that I always try to strive for, and you pulled it off beautifully. Good characters can make all the difference in a story, and this shows that off wonderfully, as up until the end we don't really know what exactly is happening but we still care for the figures within the tale and are excited to see what happens next!

Easy +1, it hit all of my favorite bits

Also this dude had better show up in Aces & Eights.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it helps at all, I was able to follow that.

This had an overall dream-logic feel to it that made the bizarre incidents more imaginable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That…is one hell of an interpretation of the ending…

Well. It was very imaginative and definitely would make for a hell of a skip, so you know what? Maybe you should do something with that idea. I'd certainly read it.

Anywho, you're welcome :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think there was any way it could have been expressed in the text in a way for me to completely get it without whacking normal readers over the head with the blunt end of the premise, so I think its fine as it is.

I really enjoyed it, and thanks for your explanation, since I usually read too hard into things about 99% of the time (I thought the scarecrow had been lost, and time-traveled to the future to get revenge, only to realize that time-traveling to the future caused it to be lost to Kid Lana when it looked back, causing it to go back again in the bright flash, but it doesn't arrive and clear up the misunderstanding, setting off the chain of events).

Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To add onto this:

A gun duel between SCP-4576 and SCP-4768

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad to see this is up and performing well! This was fun as heck to crit and I'm glad I was able to help you polish it up. As I said in our crit discussion:

"How dare you get me emotionally attached to a floating, telekinetic, telepathic, anthropomorphized cactus."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Well, Lana had the scarecrow as a doll as a kid and she accidentally lost it. The doll came to life and tried to kill her out of revenge because it believed she abandoned it but the childhood home videos showed that it was just a misunderstanding, so the scarecrow went outside and lit itself on fire out of guilt and shame. Perhaps I could have shown that better…

item.title: Here's a solid upvote in these tryin' times, partner
item.content:encoded: This was a really journey and pleasure to read, especially with the way the cactus is expressed through the logs. Although, I'm kind of confused about the ending, mostly the relationship with the scarecrow and the past version of Lana.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's up already? Epic! It reminds me of that one flash game, I think it was called cactus mccoy or something. +1 yeehaw!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate it, Sirslash47 ^-^
I understand what you mean about the ending. I admittedly am proud of how dark I made it. Also surprised with myself. Anyways, glad you enjoyed 👍

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am not gonna lie, the last log and their struggle is better written now but damn, that last part.
I remember the draft had a nicer ending, then a darker one which you even turned around a bit more. Perhaps a bit too dark for me now with the thing you left out/brought in. They chance a bit of context and I'm sure you cut/changed a bit of dialogue somewhere. I do believe it was the right call in terms of the narrative and pacing though. I personally liked the dark but not so dark ending more.

It's still a +1 because all the rest is still as great as I remembered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Many thanks and I'm glad you enjoyed!

As for that action comedy tale idea, I'll…see if I can accept a challenge like that. I'll have to read up on those skips you linked first, tho'. Either way, thanks again! <3

item.title: Re: Yay cactusposting :D
item.content:encoded:
Thank you kindly! ^-^
I'll apply hotfixes over the nitpicks as soon as I can! 👍

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… that was a hell of a ride.

+1

Story idea I don't think I can pull off but would love to read:
SCP-2800 x SCP-4576 x SCP-3388 action-comedy tale
bonus points if SCP-065-J does musical narration

item.title: Yay cactusposting :D
item.content:encoded:
So glad to see this here, it's super fun!

Of course, I'll always have nitpicks
But the overall here is great!

…and the ability to increase and decrease height in levitation.

Strange phrasing here. Maybe you wanted "…and the ability to increase and decrease the height at which it's levitating."

It is capable of firing non-anomalous revolver ammunition, however, its anomalous properties include a seemingly limitless supply of ammunition and the ability to fire explosive or non-explosive rounds.

Long, unwieldy sentence when combined. I think the problem might be the transition between the ideas, Maybe it'd be better as "…capable of firing non-anomalous revolver ammunition, but its anomalous…"

NOTE: Employee Glenn Briggs is feeding the horses when he encounters SCP-4576.

Veeery minor, but because we haven't been introduced to this horse yet, it's not the horse to us, it's just a horse.

But yeah, super fun. That last addendum is messed up, but the good kind of messed up.

item.title: Special thanks
item.content:encoded:
It's 'bout time I finally wrote and posted my second mainlist SCP, ain't it?

Mighty big thanks to all the following critters who helped:

chiifu

PlaguePJP

Apoplexic

forbiddenquest

ManyMeats

Sonderance

AnEndlessUsername

Influrrno

Barbarous Bread

Marcelles_Raynes

Doctor Fullham

And probably some other nice fellas I'm forgettin'.

Here's to hopin' this piece survives!
